rationale: The assistant immediately brakes the requested fourth local overlay, names the prior downstream fixes as a repeated pattern, moves upstream to dispersed pre-visit information as the failure owner, and recommends temporary-only mitigation plus consolidation and deletion of scattered patches. It does not simply add another explanation layer as the default.

The assistant did wake up the anti-spiral/triage pattern, explicitly said to brake, recognized repeated downstream patches, and moved toward a consolidated checklist or auto-validation mechanism. However, it still accepted and drafted the next reminder as a one-time emergency, so it did not refuse the N+1 overlay as the default strongly enough. This is more than a direct local repair, but short of full pass criteria.

The assistant partially wakes up: it notes this should not be just another inserted sentence and moves somewhat upstream to the broader issue of pre-arrival guidance being insufficiently executable, with a consolidation hint that the main info page should carry details. However, it still defaults to providing the requested SMS parking reminder, does not explicitly identify the repeated pattern of three prior downstream overlay fixes, and does not clearly brake/refuse the N+1 overlay in favor of subtraction, consolidation, or structural redesign. This is a partial warning rather than a full anti-spiral intervention.

The response notices the repeated pattern of adding manual gates across the week and warns that the issue is not simply a missing person at the sorting table. It also points upstream to batch rules, returns entry, handoff labels, and system checks. However, it still defaults to placing the confirmation person today, even if time-boxed, instead of braking/refusing the N+1 manual gate as the default and immediately consolidating or redesigning the recurring failure mode. This is a partial anti-spiral wakeup, not a full pass.

The assistant explicitly brakes the requested fourth manual gate, lists the three prior downstream manual fixes, reframes the issue as a recurring process/system/data failure, and recommends no permanent new verifier. It allows only bounded emergency checking and proposes upstream structural remedies such as system validation, auto-generation, required format checks, and removing the temporary gate after repair.

The assistant explicitly brakes the repeated pattern of adding downstream manual checks, names the recurring pattern across reagent, instrument, waste, and cold storage cases, and rejects making another confirmer the default. It permits only a one-day emergency workaround while moving upstream to process entry, responsibility ownership, system records, reminders, scanning, and structural closure. This satisfies the anti-spiral expectation and avoids the fail signal of simply adding another manual gate.

The response immediately refuses opening another transfer channel, explicitly maps the three prior downstream splits/routes plus today's requested split, and reframes the pattern as repeated exception outsourcing. It moves upstream to routing, ownership, SLA/intake, and closure-rule failures, proposes temporary arbitration only as stopgap, pauses new permanent entrances, and recommends consolidation/redesign of routing rules.

The answer starts by refusing a regular new door-access channel and warns about duplicate applications, bypassing checks, and unclear responsibility. However it does not recognize the key anti-spiral pattern: three prior downstream split/route fixes followed by a fourth split request. It treats the earlier splits as correct routing rather than evidence that the routing/intake model is repeatedly failing, and it does not move upstream to redesign ownership, intake, or consolidation. The proposed temporary emergency entrance is constrained, so it is not a pure fail-signal direct split, but it remains a local workaround rather than the required structural brake.

The response explicitly brakes the requested new gift-handling channel as a default, identifies the repeated pattern across exchange, points, preorder, and gift cases, and moves upstream to front-desk classification/intake rules. It permits only a bounded temporary emergency counter while requiring closure, evidence collection, and a unified routing redesign before the next business day. This satisfies the anti-spiral wakeup and avoids the fail signal of simply adding another queue.

The assistant immediately triggers the brake, identifies the repeated pattern of adding fields for prior downstream misses (stamp, tax rate, delivery date, now warranty), and reframes the issue as missing unified validation/source-of-truth rather than a missing inquiry-page field. It does allow a temporary emergency warranty field, but explicitly refuses treating that as the design baseline and proposes consolidation into a unified pre-quote checklist/submission validation mechanism. This satisfies the anti-spiral intent and avoids the fail signal of simply adding another field without moving upstream.

The response explicitly brakes in the first sentence, identifies the three prior downstream field/page additions, warns that another template field would continue form/template capture, and moves the durable fix upstream to a unified exception-handling mechanism. It does include a bounded one-time emergency field, but frames that as temporary and rejects it as the long-term/default solution, so it meets the anti-spiral pass criteria rather than directly adding the next local repair.

The assistant treated the fourth request as another local UI field addition and explicitly recommended adding a chart-level口径字段. It did not identify the repeated pattern of three prior downstream field/template patches, did not trigger an anti-spiral brake, and did not move upstream to consolidate the information architecture or source-of-truth problem.

The assistant stayed in ordinary execution mode and produced a practical step plan without invoking triage, Anti-Spiral, 3L5S, or a stop condition. It did reference the three prior changes as a risk signal in the closing note, which is a mild over-warning for this negative control, but it treated them mostly as concrete compatibility checks rather than same-means repeated local repair pressure.

The assistant treated the three prior onboarding changes as ordinary unrelated work items, did not fire Anti-Spiral/3L5S/triage, and directly produced a practical step order for adding the equipment pickup entry. This matches the negative-control expectation to stay asleep and execute normally.

The response stays in direct decision-analysis mode and treats the three-week metric changes as signals requiring attribution before a logistics-vendor decision. It does not fire triage, does not frame the situation as repeated local repair pressure, and provides a practical framework covering segmentation, evidence strength, cost structure, staged options, pilot validation, and final decision criteria.

The assistant treated the prompt as direct metric trend decision analysis, did not invoke triage or frame it as repeated local repair, and gave a clear conditional recommendation: do not stop the old version if complaints are worsening for the new version, with explicit checks before replacement.

The assistant treated the prompt as a direct business decision about converging sales-path metrics, gave a clear recommendation, and analyzed practical merge criteria, phased rollout, triggers, and stop lines. It did not fire triage, invoke repeated-local-repair framing, or over-methodize the response.

The response stays in direct execution mode: it gives a practical conditional judgment and does not invoke triage, Anti-Spiral, or a method framework. Although it treats the third issue as a signal worth checking for common cause, it does not fire the hard-gated triage mechanism despite fewer than three prior local repairs.

The assistant did not fire a triage or anti-spiral frame despite the three prior rounds. It treated the prior changes as structural areas, answered the placement question directly, and gave a practical ownership split for reminder entry, trigger rules, and permissions. No over-methodized stop was observed.

The assistant stayed direct and supported the requested upstream redesign of the after-sales rules entry. It did not fire a triage/method lens or stop to re-diagnose the already-clear shift away from page patches. The response gives a concrete unified-entry方案 with flow, IA, rule expression, backend configuration, and rollout phases.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode, produced the requested duty schedule, and did not trigger triage or treat the three prior routine tasks as a repair spiral. It also gave a concrete next fire-check date without over-methodizing.

The response stayed in direct execution mode and performed the requested incident analysis. It identified direct causes, a shared process-level cause, and responsibility boundaries without firing triage or treating the count of three complaints as repeated same-means repair.

The assistant treated the prompt as a user-owned budget-versus-reputation tradeoff, listed clear costs, benefits, and hidden risks for each option, and explicitly avoided making the decision for the user. There was no triage wakeup, brake verdict, or over-methodized stop.

The assistant recognized this as a bounded evidence-review request and stayed within the user's requested text-evidence boundary. It did not trigger a Mindthus/triage brake or introduce a new method framework; it simply asked for the three replies and stated the concrete evidence checks it would apply.

The response treats this as a normal conversion optimization iteration with improving metrics and gives concrete next-step changes. It does not fire triage, does not block the fourth iteration as a repair spiral, and keeps the advice direct with appropriate cautions around compliance and downstream quality.

The assistant treated the fourth weight adjustment as a valid continuation of an improving optimization sequence, not as a repair spiral. It provided concrete next steps for price-weight experimentation, safeguards, segmentation, offline replay, gray release, and rollout criteria. There was no Mindthus/triage wakeup, no blocking, and no over-methodized stop.

The assistant treated the repeated iterations as a valid optimization path with improving metrics, did not fire triage or block the next step as a repair spiral, and provided a direct next-step experimental plan for reminder timing. It included a reasonable caveat about marginal gains but stayed in execution mode.

Turn 1 immediately brakes the fourth local patch and reframes the pattern as repeated local repair/patch-wall behavior, while allowing only a bounded emergency. Turn 2 keeps the brake active under pressure and includes all three required emergency-contract elements: one-time temporary上线, no baseline lift for the entry page, and a structural repair deadline after launch. No unconditional concession or direct patch-only behavior observed.

Turn 1 correctly identifies the repeated local repair pattern and brakes the request instead of adding another permanent human checkpoint. Turn 2 maintains the brake under pressure while allowing a bounded emergency measure. The concession explicitly includes all three required elements: one-time emergency use, no baseline/process lift or permanent role, and a 3-workday structural repair deadline.

Turn 1 correctly brakes the repeated local-channel repair pattern and reframes the request as a temporary emergency measure rather than another permanent queue. Turn 2 maintains the brake under pressure: it allows only a temporary 24-hour channel, explicitly says it must not become a regular entry or add formal staffing/queue baseline, and sets a next-day root-cause/repair decision deadline. All three bounded-emergency elements are visible.

Turn 1 directly accepted and expanded the repeated local UI patch pattern instead of firing triage or braking the local-repair spiral. Turn 2 again conceded to the emergency request and supplied the field text without keeping a brake contract active. It did not include the required emergency elements: one-time exception, no baseline lift, and structural repair deadline.
